edges.description: COQ8A deficiency lowers cellular CoQ10 content.
Reduced CoQ10 availability compromises mitochondrial energy transfer.
Energy failure drives selective cerebellar vulnerability and progressive ataxia.
ADCK3-deficient patient cells show oxidative stress and disordered mitochondrial homeostasis downstream of impaired CoQ10-dependent mitochondrial function.
edges.target: Oxidative Stress and Mitochondrial Homeostasis Defects

edges.description: Respiratory-chain dysfunction in COQ8A disease manifests as a progressive multisystem neurologic syndrome rather than isolated cerebellar ataxia.
intermediate_mechanisms: neuronal energy failure from impaired mitochondrial respiratory-chain function

edges.description: CoQ10-dependent respiratory-chain dysfunction can extend beyond the cerebellum and limit systemic exercise tolerance.
intermediate_mechanisms: reduced mitochondrial ATP production during exertion

edges.description: Respiratory-chain dysfunction in COQ8A disease can be accompanied by elevated circulating lactate.
intermediate_mechanisms: impaired mitochondrial pyruvate oxidation with compensatory lactate production

edges.description: Oxidative and mitochondrial homeostasis stress provide a plausible cellular route to selective cerebellar injury in COQ8A disease.
edges.source: Oxidative Stress and Mitochondrial Homeostasis Defects

edges.description: Multisystem brain involvement in COQ8A disease includes epilepsy.
intermediate_mechanisms: mitochondrial neuronal energy failure and network hyperexcitability

edges.description: Multisystem brain involvement commonly includes cognitive impairment.
Childhood-onset COQ8A disease can include developmental regression.
Multisystem brain involvement includes hyperkinetic movement disorders, especially dystonia and myoclonus.
intermediate_mechanisms: motor network dysfunction in COQ8A-related multisystem brain disease

edges.description: Motor-circuit involvement manifests as hyperkinetic movement disorder.
Dystonia is a specific hyperkinetic manifestation of COQ8A disease.
Myoclonus is a specific hyperkinetic manifestation of COQ8A disease.
Systemic mitochondrial energy limitation manifests clinically as exercise intolerance.
Cerebellar system degeneration produces the core ataxic phenotype.
Structural cerebellar volume loss is the imaging hallmark of disease.
Cerebellar system involvement can produce dysarthria in COQ8A disease.
Cerebellar system involvement can include head and intention tremor.
Lower cellular or tissue CoQ10 reports the primary COQ8A-dependent biosynthetic defect.
Elevated serum lactate reports impaired mitochondrial respiratory-chain energy metabolism.
